title: Atmospheric Radiative Transfer Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Atmospheric Radiative Transfer Inversion: Atmospheric RT inversion: retrieve temperature, ozone, and trace gas profiles from hyperspectral satellite radiances. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.rt.disort_solver…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; cloud_contamination, nonLTE_effect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: I(nu) = integral B(nu, T(z)) * d_tau/dz * exp(-tau(nu,z)/mu) dz (Schwarzschild equation)
E.world_state_x: atmospheric temperature T(z) and trace gas concentration profiles q_i(z)
E.observation_y: top-of-atmosphere radiance spectra I(nu) from hyperspectral IR sounder

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 1D_atmospheric_profile is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); cloud_contamination dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Thermal gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: temperature retrieval < 1 K RMS for clear sky. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |cloud_contamination|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_layers ** 2 * N_nu) per Jacobian calculation per iteration
